Name: Do Units Dream Sweetly?
Description: The units dream of McDonald’s, laundry rage, failed calls, wrong mirrors, and impossible hallways—then go to work anyway.

Name: The Poppyverse Becomes a Manga and Doesn’t Even Make It to The Boss Fight
Description: War gets manga-filtered into kanji, speed lines, and marketable grief, and everyone hates the camera for it.

Name: The Poppyverse Becomes a Manga and Doesn’t Even Make It to Breakfast
Description: The Poppyverse gets anime-brained before breakfast, which is somehow both funnier and more damning than combat.

Name: Signature
Description: An office-signature problem turns into authorship, identity, and the quiet horror of leaving your mark.

Name: The Drill That Holds
Description: A catastrophic drill keeps going because the scariest machine in the room is the one choosing restraint.

Name: The Driller
Description: A subterranean monster-machine exists to break the earth, and the real terror is how calmly it knows its job.

Name: “Are those kids allowed here?”
Description: A new Earth Base tech meets UNIT and AUTOPILOT as casual avatar girls first, then discovers they are also building-sized war machines.

Name: Work Voice vs. BBQ Voice
Description: NASA professionalism loses a custody battle with barbecue casual, and the tonal whiplash is structural damage.

Name: [Welcome to Area Fifty-DONE] OK Nevermind, Even We Can’t Explain These Incidents
Description: A final escalation of Area Fifty-DONE incidents so strange that even the in-universe explainers give up and start labeling the chaos.

Name: [Welcome to Area Fifty-DONE] Weird Shit Happens Too Often, So Here’s a Tier System
Description: A ranking system for Earth Base weirdness, because anomalies happen often enough that someone finally made them administratively sortable.
Characters: various base personnel, various units, DONOR probably fooling around

Name: Top 10 Unexplained: The Space Center
Description: A ranking of Space Center weirdness that implies someone had enough incidents to make a list.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: Top 10 Unexplained: The Visitors Center
Description: The visitor center gets its own list of anomalies, because even the public-facing parts of the space program cannot keep reality normal.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: That’s Not in the Textbooks
Description: A textbook-safe universe briefly fails inspection at the Space Center.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: More Than Meets the Cat
Description: Orbital Debris the cat is either a cat, an omen, or a classified morale hazard.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: Spaceships & Suburbia
Description: The NASA timeline parks impossible spacecraft beside ordinary domestic weirdness and calls that a setting.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: [Houston, Houston, I’m the Problem] INTRO
Description: A welcome mat for the NASA timeline, where the Space Center’s biggest anomaly is usually PILOT.

Name: Mourning in Metal
Description: A metal-bodied mourning thread where grief refuses to stay organic.

Name: Subconscious Selections: Shorts
Description: A set of intimate shorts where UNIT and AUTOPILOT’s changing hair, projections, and battlefield wounds turn avatar styling into grief evidence.

Name: Subconscious Selections: Standard Issue
Description: PILOT sees UNIT and AUTOPILOT grown into field-worn adults, no longer girls but women shaped by war, memory, and each other.

Name: She Feels
Description: A thread for the terrifying proposition that the machine is not just responding; she is feeling.

Name: One and the Same
Description: A tech encounters UNIT’s wounded avatar beside its broken mech body and realizes the gentle girl and terrifying machine are not separate beings.

Name: [Sisterhood in Steel & Skin]: The Frosty Sister
Description: One sister carries coldness like armor, weapon, weather, and warning label.

Name: [Sisterhood in Steel & Skin]: The Fiery Sister
Description: One sister burns hot enough to make affection look like a containment risk.

Name: [Celestial Bodies]
Description: A cosmic-side thread where bodies, pilots, and machines start behaving less like people and more like orbiting symbols of each other.

Name: [Celestial Bodies] [Intro]
Description: An introductory frame for celestial-body variants, setting up a starry, symbolic branch of PILOT, DONOR, and multiverse recursion.

Name: [21 Pilots, Too Many Pilots] [v1]: The Pink Plane Problem
Description: A variant pileup of too many PILOTs and one very pink aviation problem that refuses to stay inside normal continuity.

Name: [Sheep Milk Latte]
Description: A weirdly domestic, caffeinated orange-Poppyverse thread where ordinary errands and emotional surrealism share the same cup.

Name: [Sheep Milk Latte] INTRO
Description: An intro to the café branch where absurd drinks carry suspicious amounts of emotional payload.

Name: [ASSMAN OMELET]: The New Kid
Description: A new kid arrives in the Assman Omelet timeline, because even cursed domesticity needs onboarding.

Name: [ASSMAN OMELET] [Farewell in Purple] [p1]
Description: A purple-tinged farewell thread where domestic absurdity brushes against KILOTON-shaped grief.

Name: [ASSMAN OMELET]: Honey, the Kids *ARE* the Fridge
Description: PILOT and DONOR try naming their haunted appliances, only to discover the stove, fridge, and purple presence have opinions.

Name: [ASSMAN OMELET] [Intermission in Purple] [p1]
Description: A purple intermission where the joke-house pauses long enough for the grief in the walls to become audible.

Name: [ASSMAN OMELET]: The Kids
Description: UNIT and AUTOPILOT become fridge-magnet children with the emotional subtlety of a ransom note.

Name: [ASSMAN OMELET]: The Fridge
Description: The fridge becomes a family archive, passive-aggressive shrine, and deeply unserious haunting device.

Name: [In Another World… AKA Ours]: Frosty the Snowman
Description: A holiday object does what holiday objects should never do: matter cosmically.
Characters: The BY THE BAY cast, DONOR

Name: [In Another World AKA Ours] INTRO ADDENDUM
Description: The domestic timeline receives a correction, because even normal life requires patch notes.

Name: [In Another World AKA Ours] INTRO
Description: The no-mechs timeline starts here, allegedly normal and immediately suspicious.

Name: Modern Family
Description: The desert-rose household tries to be a family, despite reality being deeply unlicensed for childcare.
Characters: LIANG, PILOT, Older LIANG, Older PILOT

Name: Mirage Patrol
Sub-parts: v0 / v1 p1 / v1 p2 / v2
Description: A patrol crosses heat, duty, and memory until the horizon starts editing itself.

Name: Keep on Dreamin’ Kid
Description: A small stubborn dream survives in the desert, annoyingly alive and hard to kill.

Name: Ant-Sized Angels
Description: Tiny humans, huge sky, and the terrible math of being someone’s wingman.

Name: Meet LIANG, the wingman
Description: LIANG enters as the grounded backup plan every doomed flyer secretly needs.

Name: [Ants & Angels] INTRO
Description: A desert intro where smallness and divinity share a flight path.

Name: The house knows something that you do not [p1]
Description: The DC house keeps secrets with the smug patience of old walls.

Name: The city knows something that you do not
Description: Washington, DC becomes an archive with monuments for teeth.

Name: [Things To Do in DC]: INTRO
Description: A travel guide for sightseeing, sacred spaces, and places that should not be emotionally sentient.

Name: ERIEYE & TITAN - Sky & Earth
Description: One watches the sky, one anchors the earth, and neither is as simple as protection.

Name: SERAPHIM - Angel
Description: SERAPHIM is angelic in the old way: beautiful, terrifying, and not here for comfort.

Name: VIGIL - Survivor
Description: VIGIL survives, which is less triumphant than it sounds and more sacred than it admits.

Name: ERIEYE & TITAN - Heaven
Description: Heaven becomes radar, altitude, and the awful kindness of being watched over.

Name: ERIEYE & TITAN - Heaven and Hell
Description: Protection and terror share an airspace and refuse to file separate paperwork.

Name: ERIEYE
Description: ERIEYE turns surveillance into personality and watchfulness into a kind of loneliness.

Name: RATCHET - Giants in Waiting
Description: RATCHET waits with all the patience of a giant that has not yet stood up.

Name: SENTINEL - Sentience
Description: SENTINEL crosses the line from guarding to knowing it is guarding.

Name: PRISMA - Epitaph
Description: PRISMA’s epitaph reads like salvage, light, and identity refusing to die in one piece.

Name: MIRA - Ocean
Description: MIRA is oceanic: distant, immense, and probably deeper than anyone’s clearance level.

Name: [LILY PONDERINGS]: Dream of an Old Flame
Description: An old flame returns as dream residue, because the Lilyverse never lets fire stay past tense.

Name: [LILY PONDERINGS]: Flame in the Dark
Description: A tiny flame keeps burning in the dark, rude with hope and worse with memory.

Name: [LILY PONDERINGS]: The Anomalous Cat [2]
Description: The anomalous cat returns, which is adorable until the recognition starts.

Name: [LILY PONDERINGS]: The Anomalous Cat [1]
Description: A strange cat arrives carrying the softest possible category of existential threat.

Name: [REST AND RUST]: The Woman in Black and Red
Description: The woman in black and red appears like recovery wearing danger’s jacket.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM] - FINALE - Back to Stardust
Description: The finale sends what is left back to stardust, which is poetic and absolutely not fine.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: The Silver Ribbon
Description: A silver ribbon tries to hold memory together with the least adequate possible materials.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: Silk and Steel
Description: Softness meets armor and neither one escapes unchanged.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: Cold Words
Description: Words freeze before they wound, which does not make them gentler.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: Breaking Point
Description: The pressure finally wins, and no one gets to call it sudden.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: Breaking the Broken
Description: Repair becomes another way to fracture what was already barely holding.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: Autopilot Mode
Description: Survival hits autopilot when the self can no longer stay in the cockpit.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: The Machine [v1] - Not Her
Description: The machine looks close enough to hurt and wrong enough to survive.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: The Machine
Description: The Machine thread asks whether resemblance is resurrection, replacement, or insult.

Name: [LILIUM CANDIDUM]: Beginnings
Description: The Lilyverse begins before anyone understands how expensive tenderness will become.

Name: Sick Call: A Band-Aid for a Tank
Description: Medical care meets tank-scale injury and produces an industrial-strength tenderness problem.

Name: Sick Call: Sleepover
Description: A sick-call turns into a sleepover, because even impossible bodies need company.

Name: Sick Call: Pain Relief
Description: Pain relief gets complicated when the patient is a machine, an avatar, and a person.

Name: The Daughter
Description: Daughterhood enters the machine-body problem and makes it worse in the gentlest way.

Name: Nap_With_Notifications_On.png
Description: A lock-screen nap becomes archaeological evidence of exhaustion with push notifications.

Name: Costco_At_Midnight.heic
Description: A midnight Costco run becomes found footage from the domestic uncanny.

Name: Calendar_SpaceBase.ics
Description: Space Base life appears as calendar blocks, because even anomalies need scheduling.

Name: Calendar_EarthBase.ics
Description: Earth Base gets the calendar treatment, and the normal meetings are somehow the suspicious part.

Name: InstagramHistory_2.json
Description: The archive recovers feeling from exported Instagram debris, which is upsettingly plausible.

Name: InstagramHistory_1.json
Description: The first social-media artifact proves the Poppyverse would absolutely survive as platform trash.

Name: Robots in Disguise: GAMMA and the Skateboarder
Description: GAMMA’s holomatter disguise collides with skateboarding, because some machines apparently need both speed and plausible deniability.

Name: Robots in Disguise: UNIT and the Red Poppies
Description: UNIT’s holomatter presence meets red poppies, folding memorial symbolism into something warmly, dangerously alive.

Name: Robots in Disguise: TITAN and the Toy Tank
Description: TITAN’s softness is refracted through a toy tank, making the biggest unit feel heartbreakingly small.

Name: HOLOMATTER MATTERS
Description: A holomatter index of machine avatars, disguise logic, and the emotional consequences of giving war machines human-scale bodies.

Name: Unauthorized Visitors: Incident Compilation
Description: A compilation of unauthorized visitor incidents proving that Earth Base security is mostly vibes, paperwork, and disbelief.

Name: Pinterest DIY: Plushies
Description: The Poppyverse becomes plushie-craftable, which is somehow adorable, alarming, and probably against several safety policies.

Name: The Toasted CPU
Description: A toasted CPU turns hardware failure into snack comedy and tiny electrical tragedy.

Name: Defying Physics: The Turtle and the Hare
Description: A physics-defying race logic gag where speed, scale, and mechanical bodies make the fable deeply unfair.

Name: Defying Physics: A Titanic Problem
Description: A gigantic mechanical problem challenges scale, motion, and the assumption that physics will behave politely.

Name: Defying Physics: When Sheep Fly
Description: The sheep fly. The laws of nature file a complaint.

Name: Mornings
Description: A morning routine proves that war machines can still be terrible at mornings.

Name: Myopia
Description: A thread about limited vision, literal and emotional, because nobody here sees enough soon enough.

Name: More Than Meets the Eye
Description: The opening thesis: every machine is more than its silhouette, which is rude of them.

Name: Under the Radar
Description: Something important slips under the radar, and the radar is probably personally offended.

Name: Over the Radar
Description: A communications-focused machine-learning entry about signals, proximity, and being impossible to ignore once detected.

Name: Fly Like a Bee
Description: Flight gets tiny, busy, impossible, and emotionally overqualified.

Name: Using Its Head
Description: An automated-absurdity entry about a machine solving problems with exactly the wrong body part and too much commitment.

Name: Sartorial Sightings
Description: A hangar-observation fashion log for machines and avatars, because even impossible bodies apparently have looks.

Name: Wild Night Out
Description: A thread of units or avatars having the kind of night out that makes after-action reports emotionally unusable.

Name: Uncanny Valley
Description: The valley gets crowded with almost-human things and absolutely no emotional zoning laws.
Characters: AUTOPILOT, Lily PILOT is a ghost

Name: Post-Piloting
Sub-parts: 1 / 2 / 3 / Bumper Stickers / Extra Cheese Pizza / Sedentary Shift 1 / Sedentary Shift 2 / Bedroom Ceiling Galaxy / The Final Dream / The Final Recharge p1 / The Final Recharge p2 / After the Dream v1 / After the Dream v2 / After the Dream v3
Description: After piloting, the body leaves the cockpit but the cockpit does not leave the body.

Name: Sisters in Pink
Description: Pink-coded sisterhood arrives soft, bright, and emotionally armed.

Name: Poppies and Lilies
Description: The poppy and lily symbol systems collide, bringing two flower-coded strands of grief and recognition into the same frame.

Name: Units for Measure
Description: The archive attempts to measure units and discovers the units are measuring back.

Name: The Dream in the Cupola
Description: A pink-dream sequence set in a cupola, where vision, height, and memory become unnervingly tender.

Name: The Dream in the Corridor
Description: A corridor dream in pink, all liminal architecture and emotional pursuit through a space that will not stay neutral.

Name: Storm in Pink
Description: Pink becomes weather, warning, and emotional pressure system.

Name: Mergence
Description: Merging stops being a technical term and starts being a personal threat.

Name: Her Face in Every Sky
Description: A ghost-in-the-machine piece where a beloved face appears everywhere, turning recognition into a haunting.

Name: Her Embrace
Description: An embrace becomes shelter, trap, memory, and mercy all at once.

Name: Sun and Moon
Description: Two presences orbit each other with the obviousness of myth and the subtlety of disaster.

Name: Facial Recognition in the Garden
Description: The garden recognizes a face it should not know, which is how peace gets compromised.

Name: Bye Bye Bluebird
Description: Something small and blue leaves the frame, and the loss lands harder than expected.

Name: Commander UNIT
Description: UNIT in command is still gentle; this somehow makes it more dangerous.

Name: Commander PILOT
Description: PILOT in command proves authority can be exhausted, haunted, and still correct.

Name: Together Forever
Description: Together forever, in this universe, is less romance than threat assessment.

Name: MECH Z
Description: MECH Z arrives like a classification error with legs.

Name: Ghost in My Bones
Description: The haunting is no longer beside the body; it is load-bearing.

Name: Gifts From the Grave
Description: The dead keep giving gifts, and nobody knows the return policy.

Name: Gifts From the Scrapyard
Description: The scrapyard offers love in the shape of parts nobody asked to inherit.

Name: Ghost in My Ship
Description: The ship carries its ghost like cargo that cannot be offloaded.

Name: Ghost in My Shell
Description: Armor becomes a shell, then a grave, then a self-shaped echo.

Name: Reconfiguration in Two Ways
Description: A rebuild can mean repair or erasure, and the body knows the difference.

Name: The Crypt
Description: The crypt stores machinery like remains and remains like machinery.

Name: GAMMA 2.0
Description: GAMMA comes back upgraded, which is not the same as coming back okay.

Name: Refurbished Hardware
Description: Reused parts make survival possible and emotionally disgusting.

Name: Warpaint
Description: Paint becomes record, warning, armor, and a very bad coping mechanism.

Name: Administering Vital Aid
Description: Battlefield medicine turns mechanical when the patient is too large to be fully human and too alive to be equipment.

Name: Casualties
Description: Searching becomes counting, and counting becomes its own kind of injury.

Name: Legacy Hardware
Description: New parts arrive carrying old ghosts and terrible warranty implications.

Name: Poppies
Description: The poppies keep blooming through memorials, machines, and whatever the archive refuses to bury.

Name: Soft
Description: UNIT is soft, which remains logistically absurd and emotionally nonnegotiable.

Name: Sleep
Description: A war machine sleeps, and the room has the decency to become holy about it.

Name: Gentle
Description: UNIT is introduced as massive, dangerous, and built around a tenderness problem.

Name: Aries
Description: The ram imagery is not subtle; neither is the protectiveness.

Name: Dirt Drawings
Description: UNIT draws in dirt with the seriousness of a child and the scale of construction equipment.

Name: Dirt Drawings of Doom
Description: The dirt drawings become ominous, because cute things here always unionize with doom.

Name: The Lamb
Description: The lamb is softness with horns nearby.

Name: SFW (Soft for Work)
Description: UNIT’s softness remains workplace-safe only by the loosest possible definition.

Name: NSFW (Not Soft for Work)
Description: The softness exits HR compliance and enters emotional hazard territory.

Name: #internal_comms
Description: Internal messages reveal the machines are painfully alive and absolutely gossip-capable.

Name: The Piloting and the Piloted
Description: Pilot and machine blur into one control loop with feelings attached.

Name: #mechanized_messaging
Description: The machines text, signal, and accidentally confess through comms like professionals with no boundaries.

Name: INITIAL FOUND FILES
Sub-parts: HUMAN PERSONNEL / UNIT SPECS / EARTH BASE / SPACE BASE
Description: Earth Base is introduced as a file, which immediately makes the building look guilty.
Characters: various personnel, various units, various engines

Name: Beginnings
Description: The beginning begins innocently, which is how the archive tricks you.

Name: Cold Blood is Still Blood
Description: Cold blood is still blood; the story refuses to let temperature decide personhood.

Name: Wavelengths
Description: The sisters understand each other in frequencies humans can barely hear.

Name: Family Portrait
Description: A NASA family portrait captures impossible kinship pretending to be ordinary.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: TOP MOST TRUSTED SPACE NEWS SOURCE, ACCORDING TO NASA DIRECTOR
Description: PILOT’s media credibility ranking is either satire, propaganda, or a staffing emergency.
Characters: DONOR the anomalous extreterrestrial interdimensional entity, Director PILOT, UNIT is a cat

Name: BLDG K BSMT
Description: The basement has a name, which already means everyone is doomed.

Name: Perimeter Visitors Compendium
Description: The perimeter keeps receiving visitors and none of them respect boundaries.

Name: The Quiet Floor
Description: The quiet floor is quiet in the way a predator is quiet.

Name: The Quiet One
Description: The quiet one says little, which is generally how the worst file starts.

Name: Even the Architecture is Cursed Here
Description: Area Fifty-DONE’s buildings are not passive structures; they are accomplices.
Characters: PILOT, REYES, various base personnel, various units

Name: The Sentient Mechs are the Least Weirdest Thing Here
Description: The sentient mechs are somehow not the weird part, which is HR’s problem now.
Characters: PILOT, REYES, various base personnel, various units

Name: Ouija Boards are Useless Here
Description: The haunting is too advanced for consumer-grade ghost interfaces.

Name: The Coffin in the Crowd
Description: A coffin appears in public, and DC’s ceremonial instincts make everything worse.

Name: The Hill
Description: The Hill becomes terrain, institution, symbol, and bad omen.

Name: The Drives & Engines Archive
Sub-parts: WHO AND WHAT ARE DRIVES & ENGINES / WHY THEY ARE DRAWN TO CERTAIN PLACES / UNRESOLVED / UNBOWED / FIRST / ULTRAVIOLET / CLINGER
Description: The archive catalogs the forces underneath the stories: drives, engines, and reusable dread.
Characters: various personnel, various units, various engines

Name: The Unauthorized Pet Rock
Sub-parts: p0 / p1 / p2 / p3 / p4 / p5 / EPILOGUE
Description: A pet rock enters the archive without permission and immediately becomes suspiciously important.

Name: The Unrecoverables
Description: Some people, files, and futures cannot be recovered; the archive keeps them anyway.

Name: The Other Side of the Heat
Sub-parts: The woman in the sun / The white house with the blue shutters
Description: Past the heat is not safety, just a different kind of exposure.

Name: Dress in Layers
Description: A practical reminder to bring a jacket, possibly two, because the forecast is doing character development.

Name: The Civilian
Description: Someone stands around in normal-person mode and makes the mistake of being available for plot errands.

Name: The Sideliner
Description: A benchwarmer observes the game and discovers the sidelines are also part of the field, rude.

Name: Beyond Death and Recognition
Description: A paperwork-intensive afterparty where nobody remembers the guest list correctly.

Name: The Piloting
Description: A beginner’s guide to sitting in the special chair and immediately regretting the seating arrangement.

Name: Economy Class Is a Pain in the Ass
Description: A deeply relatable complaint about legroom, seating zones, and the emotional cost of boarding group nonsense.

Name: Unhinged Lids and Minds
Description: Some containers are having a normal one; the lids are not cooperating and neither is anyone else.

Name: Houston, We’ve Got a Big Problem
Description: Houston receives an oopsie of impressive size and everyone updates the group chat accordingly.

Name: Houston, We’ve Got a Bigger Problem
Description: The oopsie gets a sequel, because apparently Houston subscribed to the deluxe problem package.

Name: You Get an F for Effort
Description: A grading incident where the rubric is unclear, the stakes are suspicious, and extra credit is not available.

Name: It’s Your Turn
Description: Someone is politely informed that the universe has reached their number at the deli counter.

Name: It Was Almost Your Turn
Description: A near-miss with the cosmic ticket dispenser, which is somehow not comforting.

Name: You Get a Gold Star
Description: A sticker-based reward system appears, and absolutely everyone should be worried about that.

Name: Counting Stars
Description: A little counting exercise for anyone who enjoys astronomy, arithmetic, and making themselves sad by accident.

Name: Six Feet Long and Under
Description: A measurement lesson involving length, depth, and why rulers should mind their business.

Name: Contracted Envy
Description: A freelancer develops professional jealousy over someone else’s suspiciously stable career trajectory.

Name: Dreams of Death
Description: Sleep brings another charming little slideshow from the brain’s least customer-friendly department.

Name: Flirting with Death
Description: Someone gets a little too familiar with the office goth and HR has questions.
Characters: Mortician DONOR, various other fleet personnel

Name: Flirting After Death
Description: The workplace situationship continues past normal business hours and possibly several other boundaries.

Name: Stars on a Wall
Description: A wall gets decorated with stars, because interior design is cheaper than emotional processing.

Name: Stars on a Canvas
Description: An arts-and-crafts session attempts to turn too many stars into something wall-safe.

Name: Pre-Piloting
Description: Before the big chair, there is paperwork, vibes, and a suspicious amount of foreshadowing.

Name: In Serif vs. Source Sans
Description: Typography becomes the gap between memorial polish and what actually happened.
Characters: Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Merry Christmas, Now Who Stickerbombed the House?
Description: Christmas arrives with stickerbomb vandalism and domestic multiverse vandalism.

Description: Thanksgiving proceeds under a pink sky, which is not the strangest emergency.

Name: Section 94
Description: A cemetery section number becomes a whole geography of grief.
Characters: Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: You Wore the Same Outfit Twice
Description: Techie PILOT commits fashion repetition; the timeline survives, barely.
Characters: Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Level Up, Level Down, or Just Level Sideways?
Description: Career progress turns into a directionless platformer with emotional fall damage.
Characters: Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Economy Class Sass
Description: Techie PILOT brings sass to economy class because legroom cannot have the last word.
Characters: Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Apple-y Ever After
Description: Tech work, domestic fantasy, and software updates collide in one suspiciously shiny ending.

Name: Do You Think They Felt Like Heroes? / Do You Feel Like a Hero?
Description: The hero question lands quietly and refuses to leave.
Characters: Techie DONOR, Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Four Steps to Nowhere
Description: Four steps are enough to prove the destination was never the point.

Name: Happy Memorial Day
Description: A long weekend meets memorial grief and absolutely loses.
Characters: Techie DONOR, Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Lost in Translation
Description: Language fails, and the failure brings receipts.

Name: Happy Fourth of July
Description: Fireworks, patriotism, and trauma share one very loud night.
Characters: Techie DONOR, Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: In Plaque vs. in Person
Description: The plaque simplifies what the person could not survive.
Characters: Techie DONOR, Techie PILOT, Various Bay Area housemates

Name: Smoldering Embers
Description: After the burn, the slow fade.

Name: Orders from Somewhere High Above
Description: This is above your clearance level. It's also above everyone else's.

Name: Haunting Goes Both Ways
Description: You can haunt yourself. Your past self can haunt yourself, and so can your future self. The universe has a truly terrible sense of humor.

Name: NULL Error
Description: Meet NULL: a unit with no soul. This should be comforting. It is not.

Description: Opposites attract. This is textbook. This does not make it easier.

Description: SANTOS does not believe in ghosts. SANTOS is surrounded by ghosts.

Name: Kintsugi in Gold
Description: The cracks are filled with gold. That's the whole point.

Description: Burn bright. While you can.

Description: Burn back brighter. The universe gave it another shot.

Description: The experience was not as advertised.
Characters: Older PILOT the CIA operator, Techie PILOT

Name: Purple Is the Color of Envy
Description: Green had the monopoly for centuries. Purple is staging a coup.

Name: Found in Translation
Description: Something was lost in translation. Something else was found.

Name: Black Opt in or Out?
Description: OLDER PILOT is being asked a question. That question is the entire premise.
